title: Corpus test runner infrastructure
description:
Build the corpus test runner so that parser development (next bead) can be test-driven against real grammar corpus expectations.

Every tree-sitter grammar has test/corpus/ directories with .txt files in a specific format: header delimiters (3+ equals signs), test name with optional attributes (:skip, :error, :platform, :language, :cst, :fail-fast), source code, divider (longest line of 3+ hyphens), and expected S-expression output.

Deliverables:
- internal/corpustest/ package (or test helper):
  - ParseCorpusFile(data []byte) ([]TestCase, error) — parse a single corpus .txt file
  - ParseCorpusDir(dir string) ([]TestCase, error) — parse all .txt files in a directory
  - TestCase struct: Name, Input []byte, Expected string (normalized S-expression), Attributes
  - TestAttributes struct: Skip, Error, Languages, CST booleans
- Parsing implementation:
  - Use the "longest divider" rule: scan all ---+ lines and use the longest as separator
  - Normalize expected output: strip ; comments, collapse whitespace, remove space before )
  - Handle :skip and :error attributes
- Comparison logic:
  - normalizeS expression(s string) string
  - containsErrorNode(node Node) bool for :error tests
- Test driver: TestCorpus(t *testing.T, lang *Language, cases []TestCase) function
- Makefile targets: fetch-test-grammars, test-corpus, test-corpus-json
- testdata/grammars.json listing grammar repos and pinned versions
- Script or Go tool to fetch/update grammar repos to testdata/grammars/

Tests:
- Parse sample corpus files (extract from tree-sitter-json test/corpus/)
- Verify attribute parsing (:skip, :error, etc.)
- Verify longest-divider rule with tricky inputs (hyphens in source code)
- Verify S-expression normalization

Reference: docs/testing-strategy.md §2, tree-sitter crates/cli/src/test.rs (corpus format spec)
